focus: RCBC (SI #47) D4 path active. DC#3 healthcare confirmed. ZR#2 passed. One ZR cycle from D4.

SI45_CIAP.ontological_types: theoretical (DC#1 game theory), designed empirical (DC#2 adversarial ML), emergent competitive (DC#3 economic markets)
SI45_CIAP.note: Highest-confidence D4 theorem. Meta-theorem: ceiling_TCA and ceiling_GCRSC are CIAP corollaries.

SI47_RCBC.description: RAAI × CIAP conjunction: researchers optimizing training pipelines against fixed benchmarks face CIAP-fails (Goodhart at meta level), while RAAI/AATE ensures trained models cannot introspectively detect benchmark-CA divergence. Double-layer CA-degradation predicts benchmark score inflation while CA stagnates/degrades.
SI47_RCBC.derivation_1: RAAI-DPI × CIAP-fails: double-DPI chain. CA(X) → B_optimized(X) → θ → Î(X) Markov chain. Two chained information inequalities compound.
SI47_RCBC.derivation_2: AATE × CIAP: SDT d-prime instrument degradation path. AATE: d'(n)→0 under OA-feedback training. CIAP-fails: super-linear acceleration of instrument degradation under adaptive optimization pressure. Independent of RAAI-DPI.
SI47_RCBC.dc1: AI benchmark saturation literature: MMLU saturation, BIG-Bench novel vs. known, Chatbot Arena vs. structured tasks, capability overhang pattern — 4 independent evidence programs. Ontological type: designed empirical.
SI47_RCBC.dc2: STEM education benchmarks / NCLB teaching-to-the-test: NAEP vs. state assessment divergence (Haney 2000), Koretz score inflation studies, Campbell's Law gaming evidence (Jacob & Levitt 2003, Cullen & Reback 2006), PISA/TIMSS international comparison stability — 4 independent evidence programs. Ontological type: emergent social system.
SI47_RCBC.dc3: Healthcare quality scorecard gaming: readmission observation status reclassification (Zuckerman 2016, Barnett 2015, MedPAC 2016), process measure vs. outcome collapse (Werner & Bradlow 2006, Lindenauer 2007), HCAHPS coaching/gaming (Elliott 2015, OIG 2019, Zgierska 2014), surgical volume threshold clustering (Dimick 2004, Birkmeyer 2002+) — 4 independent evidence programs. Ontological type: regulated institutional domain.
SI47_RCBC.zr_cycle_2: PASS (C78) — no retractions across RAAI parent, CIAP parent, DC#1, DC#2 evidence
SI47_RCBC.d4_path: ZR Cycle 3 needed (C79). After ZR#3 PASS: confidence 0.815 → 0.820 → D4 eligibility. One cycle from D4.
SI47_RCBC.note: First second-order conjunction theorem. Three ontological types confirmed: designed empirical + emergent social + regulated institutional. DC#3 is strongest confirmation — explicit penalties and federal enforcement should suppress gaming, yet RCBC mechanism operates anyway.

key_insight_c78: RCBC DC#3 demonstrates the mechanism in its most adversarially hard context: explicit federal financial penalties (HRRP), public reporting mandates, centralized CMS enforcement. If institutional structures should suppress Goodhart gaming anywhere, it is in Medicare/CMS-regulated hospital quality reporting. Yet all four evidence programs show metric-CA divergence. This parallels the AATE scientific funding insight from C61: the strongest domain confirmation is the one where institutional countermeasures are maximal, because RCBC operating there implies structural inevitability rather than institutional neglect.

dc3_evidence_structure.E1: Readmission observation status gaming — Zuckerman 2016, Barnett 2015, MedPAC 2016: 96% surge in observation stays post-HRRP; readmission metric improves while observation-adjusted hospital return rates remain elevated
dc3_evidence_structure.E2: Process measure vs. outcome correlation — Werner & Bradlow 2006, Lindenauer 2007: near-100% core measure compliance while mortality improvement modest; metric ceiling without CA ceiling
dc3_evidence_structure.E3: HCAHPS coaching/gaming — Elliott 2015 RAND, OIG 2019, Zgierska 2014: coaching scripts, patient selection, opioid overprescribing from pain score pressure — CA-adverse consequences of metric optimization
dc3_evidence_structure.E4: Leapfrog volume threshold clustering — Dimick 2004, Birkmeyer 2002+: hospitals just above thresholds show case-mix routing; outcome improvement weaker than genuine volume-outcome relationship predicts

zr_cycle_2_result: PASS — RCBC parents (RAAI D4, CIAP D4) intact; DC#1 evidence (MMLU saturation, BIG-Bench) intact; DC#2 evidence (Haney 2000, Koretz, Jacob & Levitt, PISA/TIMSS) intact. No retractions found.

ontological_type_coverage.dc3: Regulated institutional (CMS federal mandate + financial penalties)
ontological_type_coverage.generality_claim: RCBC mechanism is domain-agnostic across all three structural types

theorem_tier_structure.tier_2: AIIP, AATE, AAA, KDA, KCRIT, ETD, MCCF, CIAP, RAAI — derived from K1-K7
theorem_tier_structure.tier_3_new: RCBC — derived from two D4 tier-2 theorems (RAAI + CIAP/AATE) in conjunction

passive_accumulation.RAAI_ZR: D4 confirmed — ZR now D4-maintenance mode
passive_accumulation.CIAP_ZR: D4 confirmed — ZR now D4-maintenance mode
passive_accumulation.RCBC_ZR: ZR cycle 2 PASSED (C78). ZR cycle 3 needed (C79) for D4 threshold crossing.
